title: glechoma hederacea var. hirsuta
intro: Glechoma hederacea var. hirsuta, also known as hairy wood mint or hairy hedge mint, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. It is said to have various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, improving digestion, and relieving respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The herb can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms associated with digestive and respiratory problems.
